acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_craft_001] Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:469 contain only one direct statement that Tomas equals night access, and the later passages each introduce a new consequence or tactic. Fail if two or more of those passages still restate the same ring-key-routes logic in explanatory summary.

[chapter_01_dialogue_001] Pass if, in the revised dock exchange spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:271 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:391, each of Beto, Tomas, and Imogen has at least one line whose diction clearly marks their register: Beto reads youngest and most impulsive, Tomas reads most work/local-specific, and Imogen reads most managerial. Fail if any two of those speakers could still swap the cited lines without sounding conspicuously wrong.

[chapter_01_prose_001] Pass if only one of the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:471 still uses the explicit negation-correction formula, and the other three convey the shift without it. Fail if three or more of those targeted passages still rely on the same template.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:469 contain only one direct statement that Tomas equals night access, and the later passages each introduce a new consequence or tactic. Fail if two or more of those passages still restate the same ring-key-routes logic in explanatory summary.
findings.problem: The chapter articulates Mina's mixed desire-for-Tomas and desire-for-access logic three separate times in almost the same terms. After the first clear recognition, the later beats explain the same motive again instead of escalating it through new behavior, cost, or strategy, which dulls the chapter turn and makes the obsession feel pre-digested.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and differentiate the repeated access/desire beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:469. Let the chapter state the calculus plainly once, then use the later passages to add new pressure: a sharper moral recoil, a tactical observation about camp routines, or a concrete next-step impulse tied to staying on the island, rather than another paraphrase of 'Tomas is attractive because he is a route.'

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised dock exchange spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:271 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:391, each of Beto, Tomas, and Imogen has at least one line whose diction clearly marks their register: Beto reads youngest and most impulsive, Tomas reads most work/local-specific, and Imogen reads most managerial. Fail if any two of those speakers could still swap the cited lines without sounding conspicuously wrong.
findings.problem: Beto, Tomas, and Imogen all land on a similarly polished dry-zinger cadence in the dock material, which narrows the voice separation the style bible asks for. The scene preserves their broad roles, but the sentence music is close enough that Beto's youthful clowning, Tomas's work-grounded local dryness, and Imogen's managerial edge blur together more than they should.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retune the exchange around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:271, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:277, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:327, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:391 so each speaker's wit is rooted in their style-bible register. Push Beto toward faster, slightly overtalking camp/kitchen/dock slang; push Tomas toward clipped county-work, tide, fuel, or logistics phrasing; and let Imogen's line sound more donor-budget-compliance managerial than like another clean one-liner.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if only one of the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:471 still uses the explicit negation-correction formula, and the other three convey the shift without it. Fail if three or more of those targeted passages still rely on the same template.
findings.problem: The narration leans repeatedly on an explicit negation-and-correction template ('It was not X. It was Y.' / 'It was not only X. It was Y.'), and by the fourth pass it reads as a prose tic rather than a controlled rhetorical choice. That recurrence flattens moments that should sharpen through image or syntax alone.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the targeted beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:471 so no more than one keeps the bare negation-reset construction. In the others, let the turn arrive through fresh sensory detail, pressure, or sentence shape instead of another 'not X / but Y' correction.

findings.acceptance_test: At least 1 full-book actionable finding cites snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1 and includes measurable acceptance criteria.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's uncomfortable overlaps: desire entangled with access, wonder contaminated by biotech history, class need shaping erotic choices, and ecological harm rendered at creature scale. When a scene sits on the edge between restraint and retreat, keep the pressure visible through bodies, residue, changed behavior, or social cost. If an exact detail would make the material more embarrassing, more sensual, or more morally compromising, that is usually the detail to keep.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, ecological damage, class shame, or moral compromise are softened into tasteful fog at the moment they should bite, the scene has failed and must be rewritten at higher specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms should signal deliberate emphasis, institutional performance, or a character-specific formality shift.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compression and dilation aggressively: single-scene nocturnes, comic paperwork chapters, quiet survey chapters, domestic weather chapters, confrontational set pieces, and aftermath chapters should sit beside each other without repeating the same rhythm two chapters in a row.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, coastal, and exact without brochure prettiness; scientific and bureaucratic terms may appear when the characters would think or speak through them, but they should stay embodied by consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Mina Cordero, with free-indirect access tight enough to track bodily desire, shame, and observational obsession without collapsing into confessional summary
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple sentence music that can hold humidity, rot, and erotic charge, then cut hard into blunt compression at moments of humiliation, procedural friction, or ecological damage.
